trm:term.trm:name: Remote Store
trm:term.trm:definition: A client-side store implementation that delegates ObjectStore, RefStore, and QueryStore operations over a pluggable transport. The client correlates responses by MessageId and supports streaming for subtree operations. Transactions are managed via server-assigned TxId values.

trm:term.trm:name: Transport
trm:term.trm:definition: A bidirectional message channel between a remote store client and server. Transports send ClientMessage values and receive ServerMessage values. The abstraction is not request-response: server-initiated notifications arrive through the same receive path with no matching request. Concrete transports include WebSocket.

trm:term.trm:name: Protocol Message
trm:term.trm:definition: A typed envelope for client-server communication. ClientMessage carries a MessageId, repository name, and operation-specific payload. ServerMessage carries the matching MessageId in replies, or no ID for server-initiated notifications like RefUpdated. Messages are serialized via a codec for transmission.

trm:term.trm:name: Codec
trm:term.trm:definition: A serialization strategy that converts typed protocol messages to bytes and back. The codec is internal to a transport implementation; the abstract Transport trait operates on typed messages. The default codec is JSON via serde_json.

trm:term.trm:name: Repository Provider
trm:term.trm:definition: A server-side trait that maps repository names to store instances. Each client message carries a repository name; the server resolves it via the provider before dispatching the operation. The StaticRepositories implementation holds a fixed HashMap of stores.

trm:term.trm:name: Store Supertrait
trm:term.trm:definition: A combined trait (ObjectStore + RefStore + QueryStore) that enables trait object usage. Rust does not support multi-trait trait objects directly, so this supertrait provides a single dyn-compatible type. All store backends auto-implement it via a blanket impl.

trm:term.trm:name: RefUpdated Notification
trm:term.trm:definition: A server-initiated message broadcast to all connected clients except the originator when a ref mutation (SetRef, DeleteRef, CasRef) succeeds. Carries the repository name, ref name, old value, and new value. Enables live synchronization between clients.

pr:section.pr:title: Remote Store Protocol
pr:section.pr:shortdesc: A bidirectional messaging protocol for accessing repositories over the network, with message correlation, transaction sessions, and subtree streaming.
pr:section.pr:p: The remote store protocol defines two message enums: ClientMessage (sent by the client) and ServerMessage (sent by the server). Every client message carries a MessageId for correlation. The server echoes this ID in its reply, allowing the client to match responses to pending requests.

Messages cover three domains: ObjectStore operations (get, contains, get_by_inclusive_hash, subtree), RefStore operations (get_ref, set_ref, delete_ref, list_refs, cas_ref), and transaction lifecycle (begin, put, commit, rollback). Transaction messages after BeginTransaction use a server-assigned TxId instead of a repository name, since the server tracks which repository each transaction belongs to.

Subtree operations use multi-message streaming: the server sends one SubtreeItem per object in the DAG walk, followed by a SubtreeEnd sentinel. On error, the server sends an Error message and omits SubtreeEnd, signaling the client to close the stream.

pr:section.pr:title: Remote Store Client
pr:section.pr:shortdesc: The client-side RemoteStore implements all store traits by delegating over a Transport.
pr:section.pr:p: RemoteStore is generic over a Transport and holds shared state via Arc: the transport, a pending-request map (MessageId to oneshot sender), and a streams map (MessageId to unbounded sender for subtree items). A background reader task receives all incoming messages and dispatches them to the appropriate waiter.

For regular request-response operations, the client allocates a MessageId, registers a oneshot channel in the pending map, sends the request, and awaits the oneshot. For subtree streams, the client registers an unbounded channel in the streams map and wraps the receiver as a BoxStream. Error messages matching a stream ID are forwarded to the stream channel and close it.

RemoteTransaction holds a TxId and a reference to the transport. Its Drop implementation sends a fire-and-forget TxRollback if the transaction was not explicitly committed or rolled back, preventing server-side resource leaks.

pr:section.pr:title: Remote Store Server
pr:section.pr:shortdesc: The server dispatches client messages to store backends via a RepositoryProvider and manages connection lifecycle.
pr:section.pr:p: The server is parameterized over a RepositoryProvider, not a single store. Each incoming message carries a repository name that the server resolves to a store instance. This enables a single server to host multiple repositories.

Transaction state is tracked per-connection: a map from TxId to the underlying Transaction plus the owning ConnectionId. On disconnect, the server rolls back all transactions belonging to that connection.

For ref mutations, the server reads the current ref value before applying the change, then broadcasts a RefUpdated notification to all other connected clients. The originating connection receives only the normal reply (RefSet, RefDeleted, or CasResult).

pr:section.pr:title: WebSocket Transport
pr:section.pr:shortdesc: A concrete transport using WebSocket with configurable codec and HTTP handshake authentication.
pr:section.pr:p: The WebSocket transport uses internal channels to decouple from the tokio runtime that created the TCP socket. Background tasks handle actual WebSocket reads and writes; the Transport send/recv methods operate on mpsc channels. This design allows the transport to be used from any thread or runtime.

Authentication is configurable via two traits: WsRequestTransformer (client-side, modifies the HTTP upgrade request) and WsRequestValidator (server-side, accepts or rejects the request). BearerToken implements both traits as a standard authentication mechanism.

The server function serve_ws binds a TCP listener, accepts connections, completes the WebSocket handshake, and spawns a per-connection handler with read and write tasks. The write task merges handler responses and notification broadcasts via tokio::select.

org:concept.org:purpose: Understanding the bidirectional messaging protocol for network access to repositories.
Understanding how the client delegates store operations over a transport with message correlation and streaming.
Understanding how the server dispatches messages to store backends and manages multi-repository and multi-connection state.
Understanding the WebSocket transport with channel-based IO decoupling and configurable authentication.

rel:relation.rel:note: RemoteStore delegates all operations through a Transport.
The client constructs ClientMessage values and interprets ServerMessage replies.
Protocol messages are serialized to bytes via a Codec for transmission over a concrete transport.
The server resolves repository names to store instances via the provider.
The server broadcasts RefUpdated notifications on ref mutations.
The Store supertrait enables trait object usage in the server's RepositoryProvider, which returns Arc<dyn Store>.
WsTransport is a concrete implementation of the Transport trait.
The WebSocket transport uses a Codec to serialize messages to binary frames.

art:mapping.art:note: ClientMessage and ServerMessage enums with all variants, MessageId type alias, TxId struct.
Transport trait with send() and recv() methods.
Codec trait with encode/decode, JsonCodec implementation.
RemoteStore struct with ObjectStore, RefStore, QueryStore impls. RemoteTransaction with Transaction impl and Drop-based rollback.
Server struct with handle(), RepositoryProvider trait, StaticRepositories, RefUpdated broadcasting, connection lifecycle.
WsTransport with channel-based IO, serve_ws server function, BearerToken auth, WsRequestTransformer/Validator traits.

spec:clayers.llm:node: The remote store protocol defines ClientMessage and ServerMessage enums for network communication. Each client message carries a MessageId for correlation. Messages cover ObjectStore ops (get, contains, subtree), RefStore ops (get_ref, set_ref, cas_ref), and transaction lifecycle (begin, put, commit, rollback). Subtree uses multi-message streaming with SubtreeItem/SubtreeEnd. Server-initiated RefUpdated notifications have no MessageId.

RemoteStore<T: Transport> implements ObjectStore + RefStore + QueryStore by sending ClientMessages and awaiting ServerMessages. A background reader task dispatches responses to pending oneshot channels (for request-response) or unbounded channels (for subtree streams). Error messages matching stream IDs are forwarded and close the stream. RemoteTransaction fires a TxRollback on Drop if not committed.

Server<P: RepositoryProvider> dispatches ClientMessages to store backends. Each message carries a repo name resolved via the provider. Transactions are tracked per-connection; abandoned ones roll back on disconnect. Ref mutations broadcast RefUpdated to other connections. The handle() method returns Vec<ServerMessage> for batched sending.

WsTransport uses internal mpsc channels to decouple from the creating runtime. Background tasks handle actual WS IO; send() and recv() are channel operations. Authentication via WsRequestTransformer (client) and WsRequestValidator (server). BearerToken implements both. serve_ws() binds TCP, accepts connections, spawns per-connection handlers with read/write task pairs merged via tokio::select.

A client-side store that delegates ObjectStore/RefStore/QueryStore operations over a pluggable Transport. Correlates responses by MessageId. Supports streaming subtrees and server-assigned TxId transaction sessions.
Bidirectional message channel: send(ClientMessage), recv() -> ServerMessage. Not request-response; server-initiated messages (RefUpdated) arrive through recv() with no matching request.

ClientMessage and ServerMessage enums. Client messages carry MessageId + repo + operation. Server replies echo the MessageId. Notifications (RefUpdated, TransactionTerminated) have no MessageId. Serialized via a Codec for transport.
Serialization abstraction: encode<T: Serialize>() -> bytes, decode<T: DeserializeOwned>(bytes) -> T. Internal to transport implementations. JsonCodec is the default using serde_json.
Server-side trait mapping repo names to Arc<dyn Store>. list() returns available names. get(name) returns the store. StaticRepositories holds a fixed HashMap.
Combined trait: ObjectStore + RefStore + QueryStore. Enables Arc<dyn Store> for the RepositoryProvider. Blanket impl means all existing backends auto-implement it.
Server-initiated notification sent to all connected clients except the originator when a ref mutation succeeds. Carries repo, ref name, old value, new value. Enables live sync between clients.

trm:term.trm:name: Server Config
trm:term.trm:definition: A YAML file specifying the listen address, authorized users (name + bearer token), and named repositories. Each repository has a path that is either a local SQLite file or a ws:// URL to an upstream server (enabling daisy-chaining). The server watches the config file for changes and hot-reloads repos and users without restarting.

trm:term.trm:name: Config Hot-Reload
trm:term.trm:definition: The server watches its YAML config file using the notify crate. On change, it debounces events (200ms), re-parses the file, rebuilds the repository map and token set. If parsing fails, the old config is kept. Existing connections stay alive; new requests see the updated state.

trm:term.trm:name: Daisy-Chain
trm:term.trm:definition: A server configuration where a named repository's path is a ws:// URL pointing to another server. The local server acts as a transparent proxy: reads, writes, and ref operations pass through to the upstream. There is no local persistence; if the upstream is unreachable, operations fail.

trm:term.trm:name: URL Dispatch
trm:term.trm:definition: The client-side mechanism that detects ws:// or wss:// URLs and routes clone, push, and pull operations through WsTransport instead of local SqliteStore. The URL path component identifies the repository name. Anything without a scheme is treated as a local file path.

pr:section.pr:title: Serve Command
pr:section.pr:shortdesc: The CLI serve command starts a WebSocket server from a YAML configuration file.
pr:section.pr:p: The clayers serve config.yaml command reads the config, opens each repository backend (SQLite or upstream WebSocket), builds a multi-token validator from the users list, and starts a WebSocket server. The server dispatches incoming messages to the appropriate backend via a DynamicProvider that supports hot-reload.

Authentication is connection-level: the bearer token is validated during the HTTP upgrade handshake via MultiTokenValidator. Once a connection is established, all messages on that connection are authorized. The token set can be updated at runtime through config hot-reload.

The clayers serve init subcommand scaffolds a starter config file. It accepts --repo name:path pairs and auto-generates a cryptographic bearer token (32 bytes from the OS CSPRNG via getrandom, base64url-encoded). Output goes to stdout or a file via -o.

pr:section.pr:title: URL Dispatch
pr:section.pr:shortdesc: How the CLI detects and routes ws:// URLs for remote repository operations.
pr:section.pr:p: The CLI parses remote URLs into a RemoteTarget enum: Local (file path) or WebSocket (ws://host:port/repo). For WebSocket targets, it connects via WsTransport with optional BearerToken auth and creates a RemoteStore bound to the extracted repository name.

The URL path component is parsed client-side and passed to RemoteStore as the repo name. The WebSocket connection itself is to host:port with no path routing. The sync_refs infrastructure works identically for both local and remote stores via trait upcasting.

pr:section.pr:title: Daisy-Chain Proxy
pr:section.pr:shortdesc: Server repos can proxy to upstream WebSocket servers for transparent federation.
pr:section.pr:p: When a repository's config path starts with ws://, the server opens it as a RemoteStore connected to the upstream. All operations on that repo pass through transparently because RemoteStore implements the same Store supertrait as SqliteStore. This enables multi-hop federation where servers proxy to other servers.

org:concept.org:purpose: Understanding the CLI serve command, its config format, and how it starts a WebSocket server.
Understanding how ws:// URLs are parsed and routed to WebSocket transports for remote operations.
Understanding transparent proxy federation between servers.

rel:relation.rel:note: The serve command loads and uses the server config.
The serve command uses hot-reload for live config updates.
The serve command starts the WebSocket server.
The URL dispatch section explains the URL dispatch concept.
The daisy-chain section explains the proxy concept.
Daisy-chaining uses RemoteStore as the backend for upstream repos.

art:mapping.art:note: cmd_serve, DynamicProvider, config types, hot-reload watcher.
MultiTokenValidator: multi-user auth with hot-reloadable token set.

spec:clayers.llm:node: YAML config with listen address, users (name + bearer token), and named repos. Each repo path is either a local .db file or ws:// URL to upstream (daisy-chaining). The server watches the file and hot-reloads on change.

File watching via notify crate with 200ms debounce. On config change: re-parse YAML, rebuild repo map and token set. Parse failures keep old config. Existing connections stay alive. New requests see updated state.

A server repo whose path is ws:// proxies to another server. No local persistence. RemoteStore implements the same Store supertrait, so all operations pass through transparently. Upstream down = operations fail.

Client-side URL parsing: ws:// or wss:// routes through WsTransport + RemoteStore. Path component = repo name. No scheme = local file path. Trait upcasting lets sync_refs work identically for both backends.

CLI serve command: reads YAML config, opens backends (SQLite or upstream WS), starts serve_ws with MultiTokenValidator, watches config file for hot-reload. DynamicProvider wraps Arc<RwLock<HashMap>> for live repo map updates.
RemoteTarget enum (Local/WebSocket) parsed from URL string. open_remote() returns Box<dyn Store> for either backend. Push/pull/clone use the same sync_refs infrastructure regardless of backend type.
Server config path starting with ws:// opens as RemoteStore to upstream. Transparent proxy: same Store trait, same operations. Multi-hop federation possible. No caching or store-and-forward.
